bits.comment: Necessary otherwise the simulator throws exceptions. All values are set to True so reading any of these registers will generate an alarm.

Coil start 32769 / 16 = 2048.125, coil end 33869 / 16 = 2116.875. One coil per 2 bytes so 21845 == 0b0101010101010101 corresponds to all sensor coil registers False and all non-sensor coil registers True. In reality there are less than 50 sensors for each sector.
Necessary otherwise the simulator throws exceptions. All values are set to True so reading any of these registers will generate an alarm.

float32.comment: Set all holding registers for 50 sensors per sector to the same value. In reality there are less than 50 sensors for each sector.
Filling up the rest of the holding registers with values equal to 0. These are marked as FREE in the sensor layout document.
